questions.stem: What is the primary purpose of the Proton H2 Safety Orientation?

options.text: To introduce employees to Proton H2's executive leadership team
To provide employees and contractors with knowledge of basic Health and Safety Regulations, Responsibilities, and Safe Work Practices that apply to all Proton H2 locations
To fulfill a one-time provincial licensing requirement for new construction sites
To review Proton H2's financial policies and operational procedures

questions.rationale: As stated in blk_01_03, the purpose of the Safety Orientation is to provide employees or contractors with knowledge of basic Health and Safety Regulations, Responsibilities, and Safe Work Practices that apply to all Proton H2 locations.

questions.stem: Which of the following is one of the five outcomes a worker will be able to demonstrate upon completing this orientation?

options.text: Negotiate personal protective equipment allowances with management
Access provincial OHS legislation
Conduct independent incident investigations without supervisor approval

questions.rationale: According to blk_01_06, one of the five stated learning outcomes is that workers will be able to access provincial OHS legislation.

questions.stem: A new Proton H2 contractor asks where they can find the company's Health and Safety Policy. Based on the orientation outcomes, which response is correct?

options.text: They should not need to access the policy; their supervisor will relay all relevant rules verbally
One of the orientation outcomes is to be able to locate Proton H2's Health and Safety Policy and reference its content
The policy is confidential and only available to permanent employees after six months of service

questions.rationale: blk_01_06 lists locating and referencing Proton H2's Health and Safety Policy as the first of the five orientation outcomes, meaning all employees and contractors should be able to do this after completing orientation.

questions.stem: According to Proton H2's health and safety expectations, who is accountable for health and safety performance?

options.text: Only the site supervisor and HSE manager
Only contractors, since permanent employees are covered under a separate policy
Everyone
The provincial government occupational health and safety officer assigned to the site

questions.rationale: blk_02_04 states explicitly that 'Everyone is accountable for their health and safety performance,' indicating this responsibility applies to all individuals on site.

questions.stem: A worker witnesses a near miss on a Proton H2 worksite but is unsure whether it needs to be reported since no one was hurt. What should the worker do?

options.text: Report it only if there was property damage involved
Report it immediately, because all accidents, incidents, and near misses must be reported and will be investigated
Wait until the end of the shift to discuss it with the supervisor informally

questions.rationale: blk_02_05 states that all accidents, incidents, and near misses must be reported immediately and will be investigated, which includes near misses regardless of whether injury occurred.

questions.stem: Which of the following is one of the HSE commitments outlined in Proton H2's Health and Safety Policy?

options.text: Limit safety training to supervisors and management only
Comply with all applicable HSE legislation
Delegate environmental protection responsibilities solely to government regulators
Conduct safety audits only when requested by a client

questions.rationale: blk_02_09 lists 'Comply with all applicable HSE legislation' as one of the HSE commitments identified in Proton H2's Health and Safety Policy.

questions.stem: Where can the Proton H2 Health, Safety and Environmental (HSE) Policy be found?

options.text: At the beginning of the Health and Safety Program Manual
Posted on the provincial OH&S government website
In the employee payroll and benefits handbook

questions.rationale: blk_02_10 states that the Proton H2 HSE Policy can be found at the beginning of the Health and Safety Program Manual.

questions.stem: According to the hierarchy of controls used during risk assessments, which control type is the MOST preferred method for addressing a workplace hazard?

options.text: Personal protective equipment (PPE)
Administrative controls such as changes in procedures
Eliminating the hazard completely
Substituting something to lessen the identified hazard

questions.rationale: blk_03_02 describes the hierarchy of controls in order from most to least preferred; elimination of the hazard completely is listed first, making it the most preferred control method.

questions.stem: Which of the following is an example of a PPE control as described in the hierarchy of controls?

options.text: Installing a physical guard or barrier around a piece of equipment
Providing workers with gloves, safety glasses, hard hats, and steel-toed boots
Changing the work procedure to reduce exposure to the hazard

questions.rationale: blk_03_02 defines PPE controls as providing protective equipment such as gloves, safety glasses, hard hats, and steel-toed boots to create a barrier between the worker and hazards.

questions.stem: A risk assessment identifies a chemical exposure hazard. Engineering controls are not feasible and the process cannot be changed. According to the hierarchy of controls, what should be applied next?

options.text: PPE, since it is the next control in the hierarchy after administrative controls and serves as the last line of defense
Eliminate all work in the area permanently
File a report with the provincial regulator and wait for guidance before proceeding

questions.rationale: blk_03_02 lists the hierarchy of controls from most to least preferred as: elimination, substitution, engineering controls, administrative controls, and finally PPE — so when higher-order controls are not feasible, PPE is applied as the last protective measure.

questions.stem: Under provincial OH&S legislation, what is the responsibility of workers regarding health and safety?

options.text: Workers only need to follow safety rules when directly observed by their supervisor
Workers must take reasonable care and attention to protect the health and safety of all workers and others in or near where Proton H2 and its contractors are working
Workers are responsible only for their own personal safety, not the safety of others

questions.rationale: blk_04_03 states that workers must take reasonable care and attention to protect the health and safety of all workers and others in or in the vicinity of work being done by Proton H2 and its contractors.

questions.stem: What should a worker do in the event of an emergency at a Proton H2 worksite?

options.text: Contact the nearest provincial emergency services office during business hours only
Send an email to the head office in Calgary and await a response
Contact the 24-Hour Emergency Number: 1 (877) 000 0000
Wait for the site supervisor to arrive before taking any action

questions.rationale: blk_05_03 states that in the event of an emergency, workers must contact the 24-Hour Emergency Number: 1 (877) 000 0000, which is also confirmed in blk_05_02.
